analysis:
Port 22: SSH exposed. Verify key-only auth, disable root login, patch OpenSSH, and enforce brute-force controls.
Port 80: HTTP exposed. Enforce HTTPS redirect, review server banners, and test for common web misconfigurations.
Port 443: HTTP exposed. Enforce HTTPS redirect, review server banners, and test for common web misconfigurations.
Port 3306: MySQL exposed externally. Restrict network access and avoid internet-facing DB ports.

Port 21: FTP exposed in plaintext. Replace with SFTP/FTPS and disable anonymous access.
Port 23: Telnet exposed and insecure. Disable immediately and migrate to SSH.
Port 445: SMB exposed. Ensure SMBv1 is disabled, patch aggressively, and restrict by IP.
